reportSubtitle: Clean Data Baseline & Strategic Pivot Recommendation
summary:
This is our first full week of real Google Search data after removing Search Partners (which were consuming 88% of clicks with junk traffic). The picture is now clear: we're getting the right people to the page, but the page isn't converting them.

CPC jumped from $1.17 to $5.33 — not because costs went up, but because we eliminated the cheap garbage clicks from Search Partners that were masking the true cost.

Across 89 clicks on our two highest-intent campaigns (Competitor Conquest + Integration Intent), we got 0 conversions. These are people searching for Cvent alternatives and CRM event integrations — exactly the right audience. The landing page's CRM-only messaging and external Cal.com booking link aren't converting them.

We also paused 2 underperforming campaigns, added 17 negative keywords to stop ~35% wasted spend, and consolidated budget into 3 active campaigns at $90/day.

We've completed the diagnostic phase — Search Partners eliminated, keywords cleaned across 3 rounds of negatives, underperformers paused. The next phase requires landing page control and a tighter strategic focus, detailed in our recommendation below.

metrics.label: Search Ad Spend
metrics.note: Down from $691. Two campaigns paused on 4/2 — budget consolidated into top 3.

metrics.label: Conversions
metrics.note: Second consecutive week at 0. But this is the first week of clean Google Search traffic.

metrics.label: Avg CPC
metrics.note: Up from $1.17. Search Partners were delivering junk clicks at ~$0.50-1.00. With those off, we're now seeing true Google Search pricing.

funnel.title: Ad-to-Demo Funnel (Search Only)
funnel.notes: 124 real Google Search clicks, 0 conversions. The 89 clicks from Competitor Conquest and Integration Intent are the most telling — these are high-intent searches from people comparing platforms and looking for CRM integrations. They're clicking the ad, reaching the page, and leaving without booking.

data.note: Tracked conversions by week. Weeks 1-2 were largely Search Partner noise — only 1 verified real lead (Kait Pulliam) across all 4 weeks. Decline correlates with eliminating junk traffic sources.

charts.title: Average CPC Trend — The Real Cost Emerging

data.note: CPC jumped 4.5x because we removed the cheap junk clicks. $5-7 is what B2B event management keywords actually cost on Google Search.

growthTrajectory.caption: Initial tests revealed needed pivots. We eliminated junk traffic sources, cleaned keywords, and paused underperformers. We adjust strategy, then recover stronger. This is normal for complex B2B audiences.

findings.title: Search Partners Removal Reveals True Economics
findings.description: CPC jumped from $1.17 to $5.33 because we eliminated the artificially cheap Search Partner clicks that were 88% of traffic. This is what Google Search actually costs for these terms. At $90/day, we're getting ~13-15 real clicks per day across all campaigns. Each click now matters significantly more.

findings.title: 89 High-Intent Clicks, 0 Conversions — The Landing Page Gap
findings.description: Competitor Conquest and Integration Intent represent the best possible traffic for EventCombo. Zero conversions from 89 clicks over a full week points to a possible conversion problem. The current landing page targets integration-focused CRM terms, and we could test a form in addition to demo bookings.

findings.title: Still wasting spend on irrelevant queries
findings.description: Search term audit found branded 'eventcombo' queries (~$101, people who are already searching for you), zoom/scheduling terms (~$62), and irrelevant competitors (~$31) consuming over a third of budget. 17 negative keywords deployed on 4/2.

findings.title: Event Registration & ROI Analytics Paused
findings.description: Both campaigns ran for 4 weeks with 0 conversions total. Budget redistributed to the three campaigns with strategic value.

actions.title: Deployed 17 Negative Keywords (Round 3)
actions.description: Blocked branded queries, wrong-category terms (zoom, scheduling), and irrelevant competitors. Applied across active campaigns with CRM terms kept available for Integration Intent only. Done 4/2.

actions.title: Paused Underperforming Campaigns
actions.description: Event Registration and ROI & Analytics paused on 4/2. Zero conversions across all 4 weeks, irrelevant search terms, Google flagging them as poorly targeted. Budget consolidated into 3 active campaigns at $90/day.

actions.title: Conducted SEMrush Competitive Deep Dive
actions.description: Full analysis of EventCombo's market position, competitor ad strategies, keyword opportunity mapping, and industry vertical research. Findings inform the strategic pivot recommendation below.

recommendation.title: Phase 2: Landing Page Control + Industry Vertical Test
recommendation.context: We've completed the diagnostic phase. Over 4 weeks we've eliminated junk traffic sources, cleaned keywords across 3 rounds, paused underperformers, and established a clean-data baseline. The campaigns are reaching the right audience — the conversion gap is on the landing page side. Phase 2 requires two changes to unlock results.

changes.title: Landing Page Control – Focus on Conversions
changes.description: We'd like to build and manage landing pages on a subdomain (e.g., demo.eventcombo.com) so we can test messaging, iterate quickly, and implement a lead capture form instead of direct booking.
changes.impact: Even a 2-3% form conversion rate on 89 high-intent weekly clicks = 2-3 leads/week vs 0 today.

changes.title: Add Healthcare/Pharma Industry Campaign
changes.description: SEMrush research found that 'pharmaceutical event management' and 'healthcare event management' have virtually zero advertiser competition. EventCombo has real case studies in this space, so a dedicated campaign with a healthcare-focused landing page would test whether industry-specific targeting converts better than broad event management terms.
changes.impact: Low volume but extremely low competition and high relevance. Would fund by trimming Competitor Conquest from $45 to $30/day.

recommendation.timeline: Landing pages live within 1 week of approval. Healthcare campaign can launch same week. Recommend 3-week test period before assessing results.
whatWeNeed: Permission to build and manage landing pages (subdomain or separate hosting)
Approval to add healthcare/pharma campaign using Sharp Healthcare case study
Any additional healthcare/pharma client stories or data we can feature

nextSteps: Monitor Week 5 (Apr 4-10) with new negatives and consolidated budget for cleaner baseline data
If landing page approved: build Competitor Conquest page first (highest budget, highest intent), then Healthcare/Pharma page
3-week checkpoint after Phase 2 launches to assess whether Google Ads is viable for EventCombo at current budget level
